apply Ideal.Quotient.lift_apply_mk
  <;> simp [Ideal.Quotient.mk, Ideal.Quotient.mk']
  <;>
  simp_all [RingHom.map_zero, Ideal.mem_ker]
  <;>
  aesop

rw [Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk]
  <;>
  simp [f.map_one]

rw [Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk]
  <;>
  simp [Ideal.mem_ker]
  <;>
  aesop

rw [show kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x from by
    calc
      kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (fun x _ => by simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]) (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) := by rfl
      _ = f x := by
        apply Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk
        <;> simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]
        ]

rw [show kerLift f = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (fun x ↦ by
      simp [Ideal.mem_ker]) by rfl]
  <;>
  simp [Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk]

have h₁ : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
    rw [show kerLift f = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f fun x hx => by
      have h₂ : f x = 0 := by
        have h₃ : x ∈ ker f := hx
        have h₄ : f x = 0 := by
          simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]
        exact h₄
      exact h₂] <;>
    simp [Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk]
    <;>
    simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]
  exact h₁

have h₁ : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (by
    intro x hx
    simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]
    ) (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) := rfl
  rw [h₁]
  <;> simp [Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk]
  <;>
  aesop

have h : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
    rw [show kerLift f = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (by
      intro x hx
      simp [hx, Ideal.mem_ker] at hx ⊢
      <;> simp_all [map_zero]
    ) by rfl]
    <;> simp [Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk]
  exact h

have h_main : (@GlimpseOfLean.kerLift R inst✝¹ S inst✝ f) (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
    rw [show kerLift f = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (by
      intro x hx
      simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]
      <;> aesop) by rfl]
    -- Use the property that the lift of the quotient ring to S evaluates to f(x) when applied to the image of x in the quotient ring.
    rw [Ideal.Quotient.lift_apply]
    <;> simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]
    <;> aesop
  
  exact h_main

have h_main : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
    rw [show kerLift f = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (by
      intro x hx
      simpa using hx) by rfl]
    apply Ideal.Quotient.lift_apply
  exact h_main

rw [show kerLift f = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (fun x ↦ by
    simp [Ideal.mem_ker]
    <;> aesop) by rfl]
  rw [Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk]
  <;> simp [Ideal.mem_ker]
  <;> aesop
exact ideals.t quotReductionLift_coe f _ rfl

have h : (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x : R ⧸ ker f) = (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x : R ⧸ ker f) := rfl
  calc
    kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (by intros x hx; simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]) (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) := rfl
    _ = f x := by
      -- Use the definition of the quotient homomorphism to simplify the expression
      have h₂ : Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (by intros x hx; simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]) (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
        have h₃ : Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (by intros x hx; simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]) (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
          apply Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk
        rw [h₃]
      rw [h₂]

have h : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f fun x ↦ by
    simp [Ideal.mem_ker, RingHom.map_zero]
  rw [h]
  apply Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk

have h₁ : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) := by
    rfl
  
  have h₂ : Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
    rw [Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk]
    <;> simp [f.map_one]
  
  have h₃ : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
    rw [h₁]
    rw [h₂]
  
  exact h₃

have h := (Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk (ker f) f (by
    -- Prove that for all a ∈ ker f, f a = 0
    intro a ha
    rw [mem_ker] at ha
    simp_all [RingHom.map_zero]
  ) : Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f _ (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x)
  sim_s [kerLift] at h ⊢
  <;> exact h

have h : (Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f fun x ↦ by
    simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker, map_zero]
  ) (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
    apply Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk
  simpa [kerLift] using h

have h : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
    have h₁ : (kerLift f) = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (by
      intro x hx
      have h₂ : f x = 0 := by
        have h₃ : x ∈ ker f := hx
        have h₄ : f x = 0 := by
          simpa [Ideal.mem_ker] using h₃
        exact h₄
      exact h₂
    ) := rfl
    rw [h₁]
    apply Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk
  exact h
successful_attempts:
have h : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
    apply Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk
  exact h

have h1 : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
    apply Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk
  exact h1

rw [show (kerLift f) (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x by
    apply Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk]
  <;> rfl

have h₁ : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) = f x := by
    simp [kerLift, Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk]
    <;>
    simp_all [Ideal.Quotient.mk_eq_mk_iff_sub_mem]
    <;>
    ring_nf
    <;>
    simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]
    <;>
    try { aesop }
    <;>
    try { simp_all [sub_eq_add_neg] }
    <;>
    try { linarith }
  
  exact h₁

rw [kerLift]
  -- Use the property of the quotient lift that it respects the projection map
  have h : Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x := rfl
  rw [h]
  -- Apply the lift property to get the result
  <;> simp [Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk]
  <;> aesop
